<question>
1. a is ivory.
2. a is south.

23. If c is south and c is cedar, then d is harbor.
24. If d is harbor, then d is north.
25. If c is west and c is maple, then d is willow.

27. If d is west and d is granite, then e is lime.
28. If e is lime, then e is west.
29. If d is west and d is pearl, then e is olive.
30. If e is olive, then e is north.
31. If d is north and d is harbor, then e is birch.
32. If e is birch, then e is east.
33. If d is west and d is willow, then e is elm.
34. If e is elm, then e is east.
Which state applies to e?
</question>
